text:
Tom Daley is set to compete at a fifth Summer Olympics after being named in the Great Britain team for the Paris Games.

The 29-year-old, who stepped away from the sport following the Tokyo Olympics but decided to return last year, is set to defend his gold medal in the men's 10m synchronised platform event.

Daley will be diving with team mate Noah Williams, with the pair having won silver at the world championships in Doha in February as well as a World Cup gold medal in Berlin in March.

"There is a fantastic mix of youth and experience within the squad, and I am delighted to welcome Olympic Champions Jack (Laugher) and Tom back," Team GB's Chef de Mission Mark England said in a statement.

"Congratulations to Tom in particular who becomes the first British diver to compete at five Olympic Games – a remarkable achievement."

Daley first competed at the Olympics as a -14-year-old in 2008 and won individual bronze medals at London and Tokyo, as well a bronze in the 10m synchronised event in Rio 2016.

title: Daley targets fifth Olympic medal at Paris Games
description: Tom Daley is set to compete at a fifth Summer Olympics after being named in the Great Britain team for the Paris Games.
